From Washington Square Park to the West Village, our team serves all neighborhoods with fast, reliable service tailored to the area's unique residential properties.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Greenwich Village home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool or silk clothing, webbing in closet corners, adult moths flying near wardrobes, and larvae or cocoons on fabric surfaces. You may also notice damage to rugs, upholstery, or other natural fiber items. Our inspectors can identify the specific moth species and extent of infestation during an initial assessment.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in Greenwich Village?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Greenwich Village range from $550 for minor infestations in small apartments to $5,000 for severe cases in larger townhouses or multi-level homes. Pricing depends on the size of your property, severity of infestation, number of treatment visits required, and whether fumigation or heat treatment is necessary. We provide free estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Are clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in my Village apartment?
faqs.answer: Yes, we prioritize safe, low-toxicity treatments that are effective against clothes moths while being safe for your family and pets. We use targeted applications in closets and storage areas, along with pheromone traps and heat treatments when appropriate. Our technicians will provide specific safety instructions, but most treatments allow you to remain in your home with minimal disruption.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Greenwich Village home?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing seasonal clothing in airtight containers, dry cleaning items before storage, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and maintaining low humidity levels. We also offer follow-up inspections and preventative treatments to ensure long-term protection for your valuable textiles and garments.
